_comment: Browser globals declared in the oracle's lint environment so svelte/no-top-level-browser-globals is actually exercised. eslint-plugin-svelte's flat/base config declares NO browser globals, so without this the rule's scope-based ReferenceTracker finds zero references and never fires — making every rsvelte finding look like a false positive. This curated set MUST match BROWSER_GLOBALS in crates/rsvelte_lint/src/rules/no_top_level_browser_globals.rs so both engines test the identical, collision-free environment. (The full globals.browser set is intentionally NOT used: it contains common identifiers like `name`/`event`/`length` that rsvelte's name-based matcher cannot distinguish from local bindings without full ESLint-style scope resolution — see docs/lint-corpus-remaining-work.md.)
